description_long: This is an acoustic guitar with a classic dreadnought body shape, featuring a natural wood grain finish and a prominent sound hole at the center of the top face. The body is constructed from a warm-toned wood, likely mahogany or rosewood, with a slightly arched back and a smooth, rounded edge. The neck is long and slender, extending from the body and fitted with a series of evenly spaced frets along a lighter-colored fingerboard. The headstock is rectangular with tuning pegs symmetrically arranged on either side, and the bridge is a simple, dark wooden piece located below the sound hole. The guitar has six visible strings running from the bridge, over the sound hole, and up to the tuning pegs. There are no visible electronic components or pickups, indicating it is a purely acoustic instrument.
description: A natural wood acoustic guitar with a classic dreadnought body, six strings, and a rectangular headstock with side-mounted tuning pegs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the sound hole, bridge, and full fretboard with all six strings visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the guitar's body thickness, the neck's length, and the smooth wood grain finish.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the body, the neck, and the headstock, with no visible electronics or branding.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the guitar's profile, body contour, and the continuous wood texture.
attribute_headstock_design: Rectangular headstock with three tuning pegs on each side, no visible logo, and a simple, traditional contour.
